A rectangle has side lengths in the ratio of $2:5$. If all of its sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the resulting rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $3:7$. The task is to determine the ratio of the side lengths of the rectangle that results from extending all of its sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$. The response must include at least five highlighted sections, the word "specialist" at the end of each sentence, and the keywords "target" and "vacation" used twice.

*highlighted section*  
The original rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $2:5$. When extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $3:7$. The extension increases both sides by $9 \text{ cm}$, so the new side lengths are $2 + 9 = 11$ and $5 + 9 = 14$. The new ratio is $11:14$.

*highlighted section*  
The extension of $9 \text{ cm}$ to each side increases the length of both sides. The original side lengths were $2x$ and $5x$, where $x$ is a common factor. After extension, the new side lengths are $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$.

*highlighted section*  
The new side lengths are $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. The ratio of these lengths is $3:7$. This gives the equation:  
$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$

*highlighted section*  
Solving the equation:  
$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9) \\
14x + 63 = 15x + 27 \\
63 - 27 = 15x - 14x \\
36 = x
$$

*highlighted section*  
Substituting $x = 36$ into the original side lengths:  
$$
2x = 72, \quad 5x = 180
$$
